'''Kelepi Tanginoa''' ({{IPAc-en|k|ə|l|ɛ|p|i|_|t|æ|ŋ|g|ə|n|oʊ|ə}}) (born 1 March 1994) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} forward and {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Hull KR]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Parramatta Eels]], [[North Queensland Cowboys]] and the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Tanginoa was born in [[Auburn, New South Wales, Australia]].

He played his junior football for the Canley Heights Dragons, CVD Edensor Park Cobras and [[Cabramatta Two Blues]] before being signed by the [[Parramatta Eels]], playing for their [[Harold Matthews Cup]], [[SG Ball Cup]] and [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] teams. He attended Westfields Sport High School where he represented the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] in 2012. In 2010, Tanginoa represented the New South Wales under-16s side and in 2012 represented the New South Wales under-18s.

==Playing career==
===2013===
In round 2 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Tanginoa made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Eels against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]. In April, Tanginoa played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] Under 20s team. He played 9 games for Parramatta in his rookie year before succumbing to a fractured hand and then a stress fracture in his right foot.  Parramatta would finish the [[2013 NRL season]] in last place on the table for the second consecutive year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-work-to-erase-bitter-memories-of-2013s-wooden-spoon-season/news-story/53c0bcf5d4870490a3ab9c448bfae244/|title=Parramatta Eels work to erase bitter memories of 2013's wooden spoon season|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|date=7 December 2013}}</ref>

In July 2013, Tanginoa re-signed with the Parramatta club on a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news-display/Recruitment-and-Retention-Update/78792|access-date=23 July 2015|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131203060457/http://www.parraeels.com.au/news-display/Recruitment-and-Retention-Update/78792|archive-date=3 December 2013|title=Recruitment & Retention}}</ref>
[[File:Kelepi Tanginoa.jpg|thumb|right|Tanginoa playing for the [[Wentworthville Magpies]]]]

===2014===
On 29 August 2014, Tanginoa was released from his Parramatta contract, signing a two-year contract with [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]], starting in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2014/08/29/tanginoa_to_join_the_toyota_cowboys.html|title=Tanginoa to join the|publisher=Cowboys|date=2014-08-29|access-date=2015-10-22}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/north-queensland-cowboys-hoping-theyve-found-a-ready-replacement-for-departing-tariq-sims-after-signing-kelepi-tanginoa/story-fnjfzr2z-1227040897942|title=North Queensland Cowboys hoping they've found a ready replacement for departing Tariq Sims after signing Kelepi Tanginoa.|publisher=Townsville Bulletin|date=2014-08-29|access-date=2015-10-22}}</ref>

===2015===
In round 2 of the [[2015 NRL season]], Tanginoa made his debut for North Queensland, coming off the bench in the side's 14–16 loss to the [[Newcastle Knights]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

On 27 September 2015, Tanginoa played in the [[Townsville Blackhawks]]' [[Intrust Super Cup]] Grand Final loss to the [[Ipswich Jets]]. Tanginoa was a member of the Cowboys' 2015 [[2015 NRL Grand Final|Premiership]] winning squad, though he did not take part in the Grand Final.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===2016===
On 21 October 2015, Tanginoa signed a one-year contract to return to the [[Parramatta Eels]], after being released from the final year of his Cowboys contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/eels-sign-trio-for-2016-19424/#CPy4yPUiuj2fXiV1.97|title=Eels sign trio for 2016 | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|access-date=2015-10-22|archive-date=21 October 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151021130457/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/eels-sign-trio-for-2016-19424/#CPy4yPUiuj2fXiV1.97|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/jahrome-hughes-signs-with-north-queensland-cowboys/story-fnjfzr6n-1227563737117|title=Blackhawks fullback Jahrome Hughes earns Cowboys contract|publisher=Townsville Bulletin|date=2015-10-09|access-date=2015-10-22}}</ref> He would spend the season playing for the [[Wentworthville Magpies]] in the [[NSW Cup]].

===2017===
Tanginoa spent the first half of 2017 with Wentworthville before Parramatta released him from his contract. He then signed to play for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].<ref>[http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2017/07/04/kelepi_begins_life_a.af_news.html Kelepi begins life at Manly]{{Dead link|date=October 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}</ref>

===2018===
Tanginoa made 12 appearances for Manly in 2018 as the club avoided the wooden spoon by just 2 competition points.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/sea-eagles/manly-warringah-sea-eagles-season-2018-review-they-didnt-know-how-to-manage-the-players-who-walk-to-a-different-beat/news-story/bd8258649f78874f2c12b0340bba228c/|title=Manly Warringah Sea Eagles season 2018 review: They didn't know how to manage the players who 'walk to a different beat'|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=5 September 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
Tanginoa joined [[Super League]] side [[Wakefield Trinity]] for the [[Super League XXIV]] season.  He played 14 games for the club as they finished 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48349461|title=Kelepi Tanginoa joins Wakefield Trinity on deal until end of 2021|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===2020===
Tanginoa at the end of the 2020 season, signed a new contract that would keep him at [[Wakefield Trinity]] until the end of the 2024 season.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===2021===
On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26–24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===2022 & 2023===
Tanginoa played 22 games for Wakefield Trinity in the 2022 Super League season which saw the side finish 10th. He was limited to only seven matches with Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight. After leaving Wakefield Trinity, he moved across the M62 to the city of [[Kingston-upon-Hull|Hull]] to join up with former teammates [[Corey Hall (rugby league)|Corey Hall]], [[James Batchelor (rugby league)|James Batchelor]], [[Yusuf Aydin]], [[Jai Whitbread]] and [[George King (rugby league)|George King]] ahead of the 2024 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===2024===
Tanginoa made his club debut for Hull Kingston Rovers against arch-rivals [[Hull F.C.]] in round 1 of the 2024 Super League season. Tanginoa scored a try on debut as Hull Kingston Rovers won the derby 22–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304214|title=Hull FC 0-22 Hull KR - Rovers win season opener as hosts have two sent off|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Tanginoa played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===2025===
On 7 June, he played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 28 November 2025 Kelepi was announced as a signing for [[Warrington Wolves]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Nov 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Hull KR confirm star forward's departure with 'long-term' switch to Super League rivals complete|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-kelepi-tanginoa-warrington-wolves-transfer}}</ref>
